title: Pronomines relative
summary: The man who..., the book that... — joining ideas with who/that/which.

teach.note: For people: le homine qui labora = the man who works.

teach.target: Le homine qui labora in le officio es mi patre.
teach.roman: le homine qui labora in le officio es mi patre
teach.english: The man who works in the office is my father.
teach.ipa: le hˈomine kˈi labˈora ˈin le ofˈitsjo es mi pˈatre

teach.target: Le libro que io lege es interessante.
teach.roman: le libro que io lege es interessante
teach.english: The book that I'm reading is interesting.
teach.note: que here is 'that/which' — the same word already known as the 'que' in 'io pensa que...', just doing a different job.
teach.ipa: le lˈibro kˈe ˈio lˈeɡe es ˌinteresˈante

grammar.title: Joining clauses with who/that/which
grammar.body:
**qui** links a clause about a person: le femina qui canta (the woman who sings).
**que** links a clause about anything, person or thing: le libro que io lege (the book that I read), le homine que tu vide (the man that you see).
**le qual** is a more formal alternative to either, common after a preposition: le persona con le qual io parlava (the person with whom I was speaking).

teach.english: who / which (formal, esp. after a preposition)

teach.target: Le amico con le qual io parlava es medico.
teach.roman: le amico con le qual io parlava es medico
teach.english: The friend with whom I was speaking is a doctor.
teach.ipa: le amˈiko kˈon le kwˈal ˈio parlˈava es mˈediko

canDo: Say 'the man who...' or 'the book that...'
Use le qual after a preposition
